repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1, “Sharp joint PNDE/TNIE partial identification under a shared proxy-null bridge fiber,” is a distinct proxy-bridge problem.

published_prior_art.cite: tian_pearl_2000_probabilities_causation gives sharp generic PN bounds; dawid_murtas_musio_2014_bounding_pc_mediation bounds PC with a complete mediator under no confounding.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue mediator-defier response-polytope result.

published_prior_art.cite: murtas_dawid_musio_2017_new_bounds_pc_mediation and dawid_humphreys_musio_2024_bound_causes_effects_mediators study mediator-informed PC bounds under substantially stronger process/no-confounding information.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 concerns proxy-null fibers, and pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 concerns ordinal principal strata; neither contains a mediator-defier PN-face strictness frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified source gives an observable iff characterization for strict contraction of PN bounds after deletion of mediator-defier response types while allowing arbitrary mediator-outcome response dependence.

fang_santos_2019_directionally_differentiable gives the general directional-delta and alternative-bootstrap theory; Zhang, Wang, and Miao (2025), “Causal attribution with confidence,” is omitted close PN-bound inference prior art.
definition-only consequence: equal feasible polytopes yield equal extrema for the same objective.

structure_flags.label: Proposal cluster
structure_flags.one_line: This is a response-type partial-identification interval problem in potential-outcome language, not a graph-native SCM identification or compatible-SCM bound; rehome it to PartialID.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem thm:pn-endpoint-limit
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits Zhang, Wang, and Miao (2025), “Causal attribution with confidence,” which develops inference for PN bounds and is closer to the proposed inference rung than the generic Fang–Santos citation alone.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand / thm:sharp-sign-free-pn / thm:sharp-monotone-pn
soundness_flags.one_line: The declared target Pr(Y_0=0 | X=1,Y=1) uses the defined mediator intervention potential Y_0, while loading c targets Pr(Y_{M_0}=0,Y_{M_1}=1); rename the target as treatment-control PN or change c and all endpoints.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition prop:already-monotone-polytope
soundness_flags.one_line: Q_0(p)=Q_1(p) immediately makes the two optimizations identical, so this is a pure definitional restatement rather than substantive open content.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem thm:pn-endpoint-limit
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The joint limit and bootstrap claim for the T-coordinate is conditional on oeq:canonical-defier-threshold first producing T as a finite directional-differentiable envelope.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional on the derived finite threshold representation, the plug-in endpoint-and-threshold map has the stated directional-delta and modified-bootstrap route.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct the estimand to PN=Pr(Y_{M_0}=0 | X=1,Y=1), explicitly reclassify the project as PartialID, and retain the response-type SCM only as a construction device.
Add Zhang, Wang, and Miao’s PN-bound inference paper and state exactly why its generic PN-bound inference neither uses the full mediator law nor produces the monotone/sign-free joint-gain map.

Drop the tautological proposition or replace it with an observable necessary-and-sufficient characterization of Q_0(p)=Q_1(p), then provide a nondegenerate worked p with both endpoint gains positive and the resulting threshold evaluation.

recommended_next_step: Revise the target notation and cluster, remove or strengthen the vacuous proposition, and repair the inference survey before returning the field-grade defier-face frontier for review.
